title: GOOD: Explicit net_pkt ownership semantics documented in lichen_l2_send/input
description: lichen_l2.c lines 217-222 and 540-557: Both TX and RX paths have explicit comments documenting who owns the net_pkt and when ownership transfers. TX: 'return 0 means we took ownership, must unref'. RX: 'on success net_recv_data takes ownership, on failure we retain and must unref'. These comments prevent double-free bugs that are common in Zephyr net_pkt code.
